title: Pool Corporation (POOL) Intrinsic Value
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Pool Corporation (POOL) compiled entirely by Gemini.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: A 5.0% growth rate is applied, balancing near-term normalization in discretionary spending with the long-term, highly reliable growth of maintenance and repair on an expanding installed pool base.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: A 9.0% discount rate reflects POOL's strong competitive position, high returns on invested capital, and relatively low risk profile as a distributor with recurring revenue streams.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A standard 2.0% terminal growth rate aligns with long-term inflation and GDP growth expectations, representing mature, steady-state operations.

thesisTitle: The Toll Road of the Backyard
thesisParagraphs: Pool Corporation operates as an incredibly durable distribution monopoly in a highly fragmented niche market. While the market often fixates on the cyclicality of new pool construction, the core of POOL's business is fundamentally non-discretionary. Once a pool is built, it requires constant maintenance—chemicals, replacement pumps, and filters. This installed base creates a predictable, recurring revenue stream that POOL effectively acts as a toll road for.

Despite its excellent business model and high returns on invested capital, generating over $309 million in free cash flow, the current valuation reflects significant optimism. A 5% growth projection applied against a 9% discount rate suggests the stock is currently overvalued, offering no margin of safety for potential macroeconomic headwinds that could further delay high-margin discretionary upgrades.
verdictFooter: Based on latest available financial data (March 2026).

faq.question: Why did Gemini project a 5% FCF growth rate for POOL?
faq.answer: Gemini modeled a 5% growth rate to reflect the company's historical ability to compound earnings through market share gains and price increases, balanced against near-term headwinds from consumer spending normalization.

faq.question: What discount rate was used for POOL's DCF?
faq.answer: A 9% discount rate was used. This reflects the high quality of the business, its strong competitive moat, and its lower relative risk as a distributor, even in a discretionary sector.

faq.question: Is it safe to rely on AI for stock valuation?
faq.answer: No. This analysis is a demonstration of AI reasoning based on a specific set of inputs and rigid formulas. It is not financial advice. AI models cannot predict weather patterns, housing market crashes, or shifts in consumer behavior.
